reasoning: Atp6v1a encodes the catalytic A subunit of the vacuolar‑type H⁺‑ATPase, which is essential for acidifying lysosomes and endosomes. Reducing Atp6v1a levels (CRISPRi knockdown) impairs lysosomal acidification, leading to lysosomal stress. This stress diminishes mTORC1 signaling on the lysosomal surface, allowing the transcription factor TFEB to translocate to the nucleus. TFEB activates the CLEAR (Coordinated Lysosomal Expression And Regulation) network, a set of genes involved in lysosomal biogenesis and function. Lipa (lysosomal acid lipase) is a known TFEB target and part of the CLEAR regulon. Consequently, loss of Atp6v1a is expected to stimulate TFEB‑mediated transcription, resulting in higher Lipa mRNA levels.
